name: SELECTIS HEALTH, INC.
business: Selectis Health, Inc. owns and operates Assisted Living Facilities, Independent Living Facilities, and Skilled Nursing Facilities across the South and Southeastern U.S., generating revenue primarily from healthcare services provided to residents and reimbursed by Medicare, Medicaid, and private pay sources.
sellsTo: Residents of senior housing and skilled nursing facilities
Private pay sources and managed care contracts

exposures.sentence: We acquire, develop, lease and manage healthcare real estate, provide financing to healthcare providers, and provide healthcare operations through our wholly-owned subsidiaries.

catalysts.sentence: In January 2026 the Company completed the sale of two of its facilities in Georgia for a purchase price of $13.2 million.

catalysts.event: Redemption of Senior Secured Promissory Notes
catalysts.sentence: All of the Senior Secured Promissory Notes were redeemed in January 2026.

catalysts.event: Repayment of Promissory Note with Southern Bank
catalysts.sentence: The Company repaid the balance outstanding on the Promissory Note in January 2026.
The loans were subsequently refinanced in February 2026 in the amounts of $2,710,624 and $2,473,684, with a new maturity date of February 17, 2027.

tamClaims.claim: National health expenditures are expected to grow 1.2 percentage points faster than GDP per year over the 2016-2025 period.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the National Health Expenditures report by the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS): (i) national health expenditures are expected to grow 1.2 percentage points faster than GDP per year over the 2016 2025 period;

tamClaims.claim: The average compounded annual growth rate for national health expenditures from 2016 through 2027 is anticipated to be 5.6%.
tamClaims.sentence: the average compounded annual growth rate for national health expenditures, over the projection period of 2016 through 2027, is anticipated to be 5.6%;

tamClaims.claim: Health spending is projected to represent 19.9% of US GDP by 2025, up from 17.8% in 2015.
tamClaims.sentence: health spending is projected to represent 19.9% of US GDP by 2025, up from 17.8% in 2015.

tamClaims.claim: Healthcare is the single largest industry in the U.S. based on Gross Domestic Product.
tamClaims.sentence: Healthcare is the single largest industry in the U.S. based on Gross Domestic Product (GDP).
